title: Service Account Inventory Reconciliation ‚Äî Unexpected SPN or Delegation Changes

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 8.6.1 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 8.6.1 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.11.54: Service Account Inventory Reconciliation ‚Äî Unexpected SPN or Delegation Changes.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.54' running on index=windows EventCode 4742,4738,5136 (Directory Service Changes for servicePrincipalName), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

spl:
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4742,4738) earliest=-7d
| where match(_raw,"(?i)servicePrincipalName|TrustedToAuthForDelegation|msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo)")
| lookup pci_service_accounts.csv SamAccountName OUTPUT pci_svc
| where pci_svc="true"
| stats earliest(_time) as first_change, values(EventCode) as codes by SamAccountName, SubjectUserName
| sort - first_change
description: Tracks modifications to Kerberos SPNs and constrained delegation on payment-related service accounts so non-human identities stay inventoried and cannot silently gain broader authentication rights.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes windows and sourcetypes WinEventLog:Security. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Maintain `pci_service_accounts.csv` from CMDB. (2) Map EventCode descriptions for auditors. (3) Require CAB for SPN edits. (4) Daily diff email to identity team. Indexes required: `index=windows`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`.
visualization: Table (changes), timeline, single value (weekly change count). Key fields: `SamAccountName`, `SubjectUserName`, `codes`, `first_change`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: CDE access from authorized payment ops, scheduled batch reconciliation, or PCI assessor activities.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=windows` EventCode 4742,4738,5136 (Directory Service Changes for `servicePrincipalName`).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Maintain `pci_service_accounts.csv` from CMDB. (2) Map EventCode descriptions for auditors. (3) Require CAB for SPN edits. (4) Daily diff email to identity team.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4742,4738) earliest=-7d
| where match(_raw,"(?i)servicePrincipalName|TrustedToAuthForDelegation|msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo)")
| lookup pci_service_accounts.csv SamAccountName OUTPUT pci_svc
| where pci_svc="true"
| stats earliest(_time) as first_change, values(EventCode) as codes by SamAccountName, SubjectUserName
| sort - first_change
```

Understanding this SPL

**Service Account Inventory Reconciliation ‚Äî Unexpected SPN or Delegation Changes (PCI DSS Req 8.2.1, 8.6.1)** ‚Äî Tracks modifications to Kerberos SPNs and constrained delegation on payment-related service accounts so non-human identities stay inventoried and cannot silently gain broader authentication rights.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=windows` EventCode 4742,4738,5136 (Directory Service Changes for `servicePrincipalName`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: windows; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Security. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=windows, sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where match(_raw,"(?i)servicePrincipalName|TrustedToAuthForDelegation|msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo)")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where pci_svc="true"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by SamAccountName, SubjectUserName** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Service Account Inventory Reconciliation ‚Äî Unexpected SPN or Delegation Changes (PCI DSS Req 8.2.1, 8.6.1)** ‚Äî Tracks modifications to Kerberos SPNs and constrained delegation on payment-related service accounts so non-human identities stay inventoried and cannot silently gain broader authentication rights.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=windows` EventCode 4742,4738,5136 (Directory Service Changes for `servicePrincipalName`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Change.All_Changes` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (changes), timeline, single value (weekly change count).
grandmaExplanation: We track modifications to Kerberos SPNs and constrained delegation on payment-related service accounts so non-human identities stay inventoried and cannot silently gain broader authentication rights. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Authentication (CIM data model).
